sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 17.57 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.17)

statement.latex: Let$r(m)=\{r+km k \}$for integers$0 r<m$. For \(r_1(m_1) r_2(m_2) = \) define the \( _{r_1(m_1),r_2(m_2)}\) as the involution which interchanges \(r_1+km_1\) and \(r_2+km_2\) for each integer \(k\) and fixes everything else. The group \({ CT}( )\) generated by all class transpositions is simple (Math. Z., { 264}, no.\,4 (2010), 927--938). Is \({ Out}({ CT}( )) = ^{n -n-1} { C}_2\)?

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
